speaker: HERO
text: Good Margaret, run thee to the parlor.

speaker: HERO
text: There shalt thou find my cousin Beatrice

speaker: HERO
text: Proposing with the Prince and Claudio.

speaker: HERO
text: Whisper her ear and tell her I and Ursula

speaker: HERO
text: Walk in the orchard, and our whole discourse

speaker: HERO
text: Is all of her. Say that thou overheardst us,

speaker: HERO
text: And bid her steal into the pleachèd bower

speaker: HERO
text: Where honeysuckles ripened by the sun

speaker: HERO
text: Forbid the sun to enter, like favorites,

speaker: HERO
text: Made proud by princes, that advance their pride

speaker: HERO
text: Against that power that bred it. There will she hide her

speaker: HERO
text: To listen our propose. This is thy office.

speaker: HERO
text: Bear thee well in it, and leave us alone.

speaker: MARGARET
text: I’ll make her come, I warrant you, presently.

speaker: HERO
text: Now, Ursula, when Beatrice doth come,

speaker: HERO
text: As we do trace this alley up and down,

speaker: HERO
text: Our talk must only be of Benedick.

speaker: HERO
text: When I do name him, let it be thy part

speaker: HERO
text: To praise him more than ever man did merit.

speaker: HERO
text: My talk to thee must be how Benedick

speaker: HERO
text: Is sick in love with Beatrice. Of this matter

speaker: HERO
text: Is little Cupid’s crafty arrow made,

speaker: HERO
text: That only wounds by hearsay. Now begin,

speaker: HERO
text: For look where Beatrice like a lapwing runs

speaker: HERO
text: Close by the ground, to hear our conference.

speaker: URSULA
text: The pleasant’st angling is to see the fish

speaker: URSULA
text: Cut with her golden oars the silver stream

speaker: URSULA
text: And greedily devour the treacherous bait.

speaker: URSULA
text: So angle we for Beatrice, who even now

speaker: URSULA
text: Is couchèd in the woodbine coverture.

speaker: URSULA
text: Fear you not my part of the dialogue.

speaker: HERO
text: Then go we near her, that her ear lose nothing

speaker: HERO
text: Of the false sweet bait that we lay for it.—

speaker: HERO
text: No, truly, Ursula, she is too disdainful.

speaker: HERO
text: I know her spirits are as coy and wild

speaker: HERO
text: As haggards of the rock.

speaker: URSULA
text: But are you sure

speaker: URSULA
text: That Benedick loves Beatrice so entirely?

speaker: HERO
text: So says the Prince and my new-trothèd lord.

speaker: URSULA
text: And did they bid you tell her of it, madam?

speaker: HERO
text: They did entreat me to acquaint her of it,

speaker: HERO
text: But I persuaded them, if they loved Benedick,

speaker: HERO
text: To wish him wrestle with affection

speaker: HERO
text: And never to let Beatrice know of it.

speaker: URSULA
text: Why did you so? Doth not the gentleman

speaker: URSULA
text: Deserve as full as fortunate a bed

speaker: URSULA
text: As ever Beatrice shall couch upon?

speaker: HERO
text: O god of love! I know he doth deserve

speaker: HERO
text: As much as may be yielded to a man,

speaker: HERO
text: But Nature never framed a woman’s heart

speaker: HERO
text: Of prouder stuff than that of Beatrice.

speaker: HERO
text: Disdain and scorn ride sparkling in her eyes,

speaker: HERO
text: Misprizing what they look on, and her wit

speaker: HERO
text: Values itself so highly that to her

speaker: HERO
text: All matter else seems weak. She cannot love,

speaker: HERO
text: Nor take no shape nor project of affection,

speaker: HERO
text: She is so self-endeared.

speaker: URSULA
text: Sure, I think so,

speaker: URSULA
text: And therefore certainly it were not good

speaker: URSULA
text: She knew his love, lest she’ll make sport at it.

speaker: HERO
text: Why, you speak truth. I never yet saw man,

speaker: HERO
text: How wise, how noble, young, how rarely featured,

speaker: HERO
text: But she would spell him backward. If fair-faced,

speaker: HERO
text: She would swear the gentleman should be her sister;

speaker: HERO
text: If black, why, Nature, drawing of an antic,

speaker: HERO
text: Made a foul blot; if tall, a lance ill-headed;

speaker: HERO
text: If low, an agate very vilely cut;

speaker: HERO
text: If speaking, why, a vane blown with all winds;

speaker: HERO
text: If silent, why, a block moved with none.

speaker: HERO
text: So turns she every man the wrong side out,

speaker: HERO
text: And never gives to truth and virtue that

speaker: HERO
text: Which simpleness and merit purchaseth.

speaker: URSULA
text: Sure, sure, such carping is not commendable.

speaker: HERO
text: No, not to be so odd and from all fashions

speaker: HERO
text: As Beatrice is cannot be commendable.

speaker: HERO
text: But who dare tell her so? If I should speak,

speaker: HERO
text: She would mock me into air. O, she would laugh me

speaker: HERO
text: Out of myself, press me to death with wit.

speaker: HERO
text: Therefore let Benedick, like covered fire,

speaker: HERO
text: Consume away in sighs, waste inwardly.

speaker: HERO
text: It were a better death than die with mocks,

speaker: HERO
text: Which is as bad as die with tickling.

speaker: URSULA
text: Yet tell her of it. Hear what she will say.

speaker: HERO
text: No, rather I will go to Benedick

speaker: HERO
text: And counsel him to fight against his passion;

speaker: HERO
text: And truly I’ll devise some honest slanders

speaker: HERO
text: To stain my cousin with. One doth not know

speaker: HERO
text: How much an ill word may empoison liking.

speaker: URSULA
text: O, do not do your cousin such a wrong!

speaker: URSULA
text: She cannot be so much without true judgment,

speaker: URSULA
text: Having so swift and excellent a wit

speaker: URSULA
text: As she is prized to have, as to refuse

speaker: URSULA
text: So rare a gentleman as Signior Benedick.

speaker: HERO
text: He is the only man of Italy,

speaker: HERO
text: Always excepted my dear Claudio.

speaker: URSULA
text: I pray you be not angry with me, madam,

speaker: URSULA
text: Speaking my fancy: Signior Benedick,

speaker: URSULA
text: For shape, for bearing, argument, and valor,

speaker: URSULA
text: Goes foremost in report through Italy.

speaker: HERO
text: Indeed, he hath an excellent good name.

speaker: URSULA
text: His excellence did earn it ere he had it.

speaker: URSULA
text: When are you married, madam?

speaker: HERO
text: Why, every day, tomorrow. Come, go in.

speaker: HERO
text: I’ll show thee some attires and have thy counsel

speaker: HERO
text: Which is the best to furnish me tomorrow.

speaker: URSULA
text: She’s limed, I warrant you. We have caught her, madam.

speaker: HERO
text: If it prove so, then loving goes by haps;

speaker: HERO
text: Some Cupid kills with arrows, some with traps.

speaker: BEATRICE
text: What fire is in mine ears? Can this be true?

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Stand I condemned for pride and scorn so much?

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Contempt, farewell, and maiden pride, adieu!

speaker: BEATRICE
text: No glory lives behind the back of such.

speaker: BEATRICE
text: And Benedick, love on; I will requite thee,

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Taming my wild heart to thy loving hand.

speaker: BEATRICE
text: If thou dost love, my kindness shall incite thee

speaker: BEATRICE
text: To bind our loves up in a holy band.

speaker: BEATRICE
text: For others say thou dost deserve, and I

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Believe it better than reportingly.
